skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
12, 11, 19, 18, x

The mean and the median of the five numbers above are equal. Which of the following is NOT a possible value of x?
rationale: Choice D is correct. Suppose x=5. Then the five numbers are 12, 11, 19, 18, 5, whose sum is 65, so the mean is 65/5=13. Ordered from least to greatest the numbers are 5, 11, 12, 18, 19, so the median is 12. Because 13 ≠ 12, the mean and median are not equal when x=5. Choices A, B, and C are incorrect; substituting any of these values for x makes the mean equal to the median: when x=15, the ordered list is 11, 12, 15, 18, 19 with mean 15 and median 15; when x=30, the ordered list is 11, 12, 18, 19, 30 with mean 18 and median 18; when x=0, the ordered list is 0, 11, 12, 18, 19 with mean 12 and median 12.
